title: Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Thrissilery Shiva Temple is a Shiva temple situated at Thrissilery neighbourhood in Wayanad, Kerala, India.
overview.description: Thrissilery Shiva Temple is a Shiva temple situated at Thrissilery neighbourhood in Wayanad, Kerala, India. This ancient temple is dedicated to Shiva. This temple is a popular pilgrim centre in Wayanad. This temple is believed to have been built about 1000 years ago by Chera kings.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thrissilery
topPlaces.description: Thrissilery is a village in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kattikkulam
topPlaces.description: Kattikkulam (Kartikulam) is a small town near Mananthavady in Wayanad district, Kerala, India. The office of the Thirunelly Gramapanchayath is located at Kartikulam.

topPlaces.name: Pazhassi Raja
topPlaces.description: Kerala Varma Pazhassi Raja (IPA: [pɐɻɐʃːi ɾaːd͡ʒɐ]) (3 January 1753 – 30 November 1805), also known as Cotiote Rajah and Pychy Rajah, was the de facto head of the Kottayam Kingdom in the Malabar region of Kerala between 1774 and 1805. His struggle against the British East India Company is known as the Cotiote War, for which he has been touted as one of the earliest freedom fighters in India.

topPlaces.name: Mananthavady
topPlaces.description: Mananthavady is a Municipality, Taluk and town in the Wayanad district of Kerala, India. The municipal boundaries are defined by Tirunelli Panchayat to the north, Kabini River to the east and south, and Thavinhal Panchayat to the west.

topPlaces.name: Appapara
topPlaces.description: Appapara or Appappara is a small village in Wayanad district of Kerala state, India.

topPlaces.name: Payyampally
topPlaces.description: Payyampally is a town in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Thirunelli Temple
topPlaces.description: Thirunelli Temple (also spelled Tirunelli) is an ancient temple dedicated to Maha Vishnu on the side of Brahmagiri hill in Kerala, India, near the border with Karnataka state. The temple is classified one among the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavate tradition.

topPlaces.name: Thalappuzha, Wayanad
topPlaces.description: Thalappuzha, Thavinhal or Thavinjal is a village in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India. It is formally known as Aranguthum Chall, meaning the place for cultural programs.

items.note: Thrissilery is a village in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.
Kattikkulam (Kartikulam) is a small town near Mananthavady in Wayanad district, Kerala, India. The office of the Thirunelly Gramapanchayath is located at Kartikulam.

Kerala Varma Pazhassi Raja (IPA: [pɐɻɐʃːi ɾaːd͡ʒɐ]) (3 January 1753 – 30 November 1805), also known as Cotiote Rajah and Pychy Rajah, was the de facto head of the Kottayam Kingdom in the Malabar region of Kerala between 1774 and 1805. His struggle against the British East India Company is known as the Cotiote War, for which he has been touted as one of the earliest freedom fighters in India.

Mananthavady is a Municipality, Taluk and town in the Wayanad district of Kerala, India. The municipal boundaries are defined by Tirunelli Panchayat to the north, Kabini River to the east and south, and Thavinhal Panchayat to the west.
Appapara or Appappara is a small village in Wayanad district of Kerala state, India.
Payyampally is a town in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India.

Thirunelli Temple (also spelled Tirunelli) is an ancient temple dedicated to Maha Vishnu on the side of Brahmagiri hill in Kerala, India, near the border with Karnataka state. The temple is classified one among the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavate tradition.
Thalappuzha, Thavinhal or Thavinjal is a village in Wayanad district in the state of Kerala, India. It is formally known as Aranguthum Chall, meaning the place for cultural programs.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (80 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (85 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thrissileri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thrissilery, Kattikkulam, Pazhassi Raja, Mananthavady, Appapara.
